comments.author: @OrlandoCoombs-i4t
comments.text: Lord have mercy, the resilience and resourcefulness of global African Diaspora is absolutely astonishing. African Slaves took the peel from a bitter green orange and extracted aromic oils from it and help create one of the iconic liquor brands on EARTH, CURACAO LIQUOR which is orange flavored liquor now popularized globally. Curacao produces the highest number of major league baseball players per capita on the planet earth. They speak Papiamento, which is a language native to Curacao with it's origins in several African languages keeping their roots and connections to the motherland alive through chattel slavery and colonialism. It's blended from the Dutch, Spanish, and Arawak languages of those people's who were natives to the island along with the African slaves and their enslavers and colonizers, along with a few African languages, never forgetting who they are or where they come from. All of these things I mention here are pinpoints of Black ingenuity, creativity, resistance, and resilience. From the Maroons in Jamaica, Guyana, Suriname, Colombia, and elsewhere in the Caribbean and South America, to the baseball players of Curacao, from Haiti, the first independent black nation in the Western Hemisphere to the MAU MAU of Kenya, From the freedom fighters against colonialism in Angola, Mozambique, and Guinea Bissau to Alonzo Herndon, the black entrepreneur who rose from being a slave in rural Georgia to one of the Atlanta's wealthiest citizens who created an insurance company that has long outlived him and exists today. And also, the young black boys of the Barry Farms housing projects in Washington DC who took old discarded pots and pans that people threw away and creating an entire new music industry entirely called GO GO MUSIC in DC which is now a global phenomenon. EU and others have become world renown GO GO MUSIC ARTISTS and even Wyclef sang about female GO GO artists. And this all came from the discarded pots and pans in a trash dump in the Barry Farms housing projects in Washington DC decades ago. I can site stories forever but no matter where we hail from on this earth, our diaspora is amazing. Every group of African people throughout the global Black Diaspora has 3 things in common that both connects and strengthen us: Our Resilience, Resourcefulness, and Resistance. And regardless of the conditions that our enemies have put upon us, we've continued to rise above them triumphantly each and everytime stronger than ever. Our Resilience, Resourcefulness, and Resistance has never died, and it never will. NEVER THAT.

comments.author: @WorldUnveiled-z8
comments.text: I didn’t expect Curaçao to have such a unique mix of island life, dry landscapes, and cultural identity. This made me want to learn more about how people actually live there.

comments.author: @CountryTravelDocumentary
comments.text: **"Curaçao looks like a tropical paradise, but here's my question: Could you actually live there for a year, or would you only want to visit? 🌴🏝 Be honest—I want to know why!"**

replies.author: @reallifeincountries
replies.text: Living there for a year sounds like a dream, but island fever is real! 🏝️ I’d say 6 months for the vibe, 6 months to travel. How about you—could you handle the slow island life for a full year?"
